CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, datecol DATE);
SELECT datecol FROM test_dates WHERE datecol > CAST('1950-01-01' AS DATE) AND datecol < CAST('2025-01-01' AS DATE);
SELECT datecol FROM test_dates WHERE datecol = CAST('2000-02-29' AS DATE);
SELECT id, datecol FROM test_dates WHERE datecol BETWEEN CAST('1998-01-01' AS DATE) AND CAST('2001-12-31' AS DATE);
SELECT id FROM test_dates WHERE datecol IN (SELECT datecol FROM test_dates WHERE datecol < CAST(NOW() AS DATE));
CREATE TABLE event_dates (event_id INT, event_date DATETIME);
SELECT ed.event_id FROM event_dates ed JOIN test_dates td ON DATE(ed.event_date) = td.datecol WHERE td.datecol > CAST('2000-01-01' AS DATE);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM event_dates WHERE event_date > CAST('2023-01-01 00:00:00' AS DATETIME);
SELECT * FROM test_dates WHERE DATEDIFF(CURDATE(), datecol) < 30;
SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF(NOW(), datecol)) AS avg_days FROM test_dates;
SELECT * FROM test_dates WHERE datecol <= CAST('2001-12-31' AS DATE) ORDER BY datecol DESC;
